title: memory use reported is wrong
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.23 (git sha1 7ce5011c24b)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

Run yosys any way you like, but so it uses more than RAM capacity at peak, needing swap.

### Expected Behavior

The final MEM: output should be the peak memory footprint required.

### Actual Behavior

What is actually reported is the peak memory footprint in RAM, it does not include swap space needed.

E.g. _End of script. Logfile hash: adee604607, CPU: user 398333.78s system 10906.60s, **MEM: 89465.26 MB** peak_

That is false. The RAM on that machine is 90GB and the process used it all, maxing at 153GB RAM+swap of memory through about 110h of cpu time, at between 50 and 100% cpu. I watched it throughout with htop. An earlier try with only 90GB+0.5GB swap enabled failed with kernel out of memory (OOM) reaper having it killed after  24h.

The problem is getrusage() in ./kernel/driver.cc, which gets the process data into ru_buffer. It's not your fault, but getrusage doesn't record peak _memory_ use (the ru_maxrss field) as the man page for getrusage vaguely claims, it records the RAM usage.

The only fix is to get the memory usage the way htop (_VIRT_ column, not _RES_) and other similar utilities do it ... which I am not going to look up but which i would guess is  via some function in a ps-misc library that polls data in /proc. 

(NB, via _ps aux_, that's the _VSZ_ column that's wanted, not the _RSS_ column, and that's likely going to be simpler code to look at for guidance).

The reason one needs the right info out is so that I _don't_ have to watch the process myself through 110h of cpu time in order to know how much swap I really can get away with next time round.
